framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link — the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.path: Build and maintain a widely-used open-source project
paths.what_it_is: Create a developer tool or library, publish it publicly on GitHub, and keep maintaining it long enough that other engineers depend on it in production.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you can design software other engineers trust with their own production systems, and sustain that trust for years — judgment no interview loop can test.

paths.path: Write in public to teach hard technical concepts clearly
paths.what_it_is: Publish blog posts, zines, or books that break down difficult systems topics (networking, performance, distributed data) in a way that makes them accessible to other engineers.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you understand a hard technical concept well enough to make it click for someone else — a distillation skill that's rarer than just knowing the material.

paths.path: Master competitive programming and algorithmic problem-solving
paths.what_it_is: Compete regularly in ranked algorithmic contests (Codeforces, ICPC, Topcoder) and reach elite, publicly verifiable rating tiers through sustained deliberate practice.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you can solve unfamiliar problems correctly and fast under real time pressure, with a public, rankable record instead of a self-reported claim.
